results.title: Quadrature rule optimization
results.summary: A compact five-node rule for estimating one-dimensional integrals, improved against a fixed public test suite. The report separates the acceptance score from direct residual errors so the gain can be inspected, replayed, and challenged.

website.card_summary: A five-node integration rule is compared against a fixed baseline, with both the acceptance score and direct residual errors exposed.

figure_captions.assets/objective-curve.svg: Best-so-far objective over the curated public trace. The acceptance score is lower-is-better and should be read as the contract used to retain candidates.
figure_captions.assets/candidate-trace.svg: Accepted quadrature rule: node positions, normalized weights, and residual error by representative integrand.

website.score_note: The objective is the evaluation contract used by the run, not a universal measure of integration quality. The scientific interpretation comes from reading it together with the residual errors and the replayable candidate.

results.title: PSPLIB J30 scheduling benchmark
results.summary: A deterministic resource-constrained project scheduling rule improved across a frozen PSPLIB J30 subset with proven optimal makespans. The public chain reduced the lower-is-better score from 14.312 to 12.087 while keeping feasibility at 100%.

website.card_summary: A scheduling dispatch rule is evaluated against PSPLIB J30 optima, reducing the governed score while preserving feasibility.

figure_captions.assets/objective-curve.svg: Best-so-far objective curve in the same paper style as the quadrature result. Lower is better; the public comparison moves from 14.312 to 12.087.
figure_captions.assets/benchmark-comparison.svg: Baseline versus accepted comparison across score, mean gap, p95 gap, valid schedules, and exact proven optima reached.
figure_captions.assets/gap-readout.svg: Accepted candidate diagnostics on the frozen 80-instance portfolio: mean gap, p95 gap, max gap, and exact proven optima reached.
figure_captions.artifacts/schedule-example.json: Sanitized real schedule readout for PSPLIB J30 instance j3025_9: selected shifted jobs, makespans, capacities, and resource-load buckets.
figure_captions.assets/tail-gap-ladder.svg: Worst-instance tail behavior for the accepted candidate, showing residual gap and makespan versus proven optimum.
figure_captions.assets/schedule-network.svg: RCPSP schedule readout: precedence network, Gantt placement, and makespan.
figure_captions.assets/resource-load.svg: Renewable-resource load profile for a feasible schedule, with the capacity line kept visible.

website.score_note: The score is the frozen evaluation contract for this public PSPLIB J30 subset. It is not a runtime benchmark and it is not a claim over all RCPSP instances.
